shell.actions.apply: Generic Apply button label — applies the current selection or settings without closing the surface. Verb, imperative.
shell.actions.cancel: Generic Cancel button label — abandons the current action and closes its dialog. Verb, imperative. Pairs with a Confirm/Save action.
shell.actions.close: Generic Close button label — dismisses the current popover, dialog, or panel without committing changes. Verb, imperative.
shell.actions.confirm: Generic Confirm button label — proceeds with the action the dialog is asking about. Verb, imperative.
shell.actions.delete: Generic Delete button label — removes the current object (soft-delete to the Bin). Verb, imperative; destructive tone.
shell.actions.reset: Generic Reset button label — returns the current setting to its default value. Verb, imperative.
shell.actions.save: Generic Save button label — commits the current edit. Verb, imperative.
shell.appGrid.empty: Empty-state text in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “No apps match.”.
shell.appGrid.gridLabel: Field label in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “All installed apps”.
shell.appGrid.menu.open: Button label in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “Open”.
shell.appGrid.menu.pin: UI string in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “Pin to dashboard”.
shell.appGrid.menu.unpin: Context-menu item in the all-apps grid that removes the app's tile from the dashboard. Imperative, short.
shell.appGrid.openAria: Accessible (screen-reader) label in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “Show all apps”.
shell.appGrid.openLabel: Field label in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “Apps”.
shell.appGrid.placeholder: Input placeholder in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “Search apps…”.
shell.appGrid.title: Heading in the dashboard app grid (launcher): “All Apps”.
shell.bin.clearSelection: Button that clears the current multi-select in the Bin (trash) overlay, deselecting all chosen objects. Imperative, very short.
shell.bin.emptiedToast.body: Toast body confirming the Bin was emptied, stating how many objects were permanently removed. {count} is the integer total removed; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.bin.emptyConfirm.body: Body of the empty-Bin confirmation dialog, stating how many objects will be permanently deleted. {count} is the integer object total in the Bin; ICU plural picks the noun form. Cautionary, not alarmist.
shell.bin.purgeManyConfirm.body: Body of the confirm dialog before permanently deleting the selected trashed objects. Warns the action is irreversible. {count} is the number selected; uses ICU plural.
shell.bin.purgeManyConfirm.title: Title of the confirm dialog before permanently deleting selected trashed objects. {count} is the number selected; uses ICU plural with # as the count.
shell.bin.purgedManyToast.body: Toast body after permanently deleting selected trashed objects. {count} is how many were removed; uses ICU plural with # as the count.
shell.bin.purgedManyToast.title: Toast title confirming selected objects were permanently deleted from the Bin. Short.
shell.bin.restoredManyToast.body: Toast body after restoring selected objects out of the Bin back to their apps. {count} is how many; uses ICU plural with # as the count.
shell.bin.restoredManyToast.title: Toast title confirming selected objects were restored from the Bin. Short.
shell.bin.selectAll: Button that selects every object currently shown in the Bin (trash) overlay. Imperative, very short.
shell.bin.selectedCount: Count pill showing how many trashed objects are currently selected in the Bin. {count} is the number; uses ICU plural with # as the count.
shell.common.loading: Generic loading placeholder shown while a surface fetches its data. Brief, neutral.
shell.dashboard.iconGrid: Accessible label for the dashboard icon grid — the spatially-navigable group of app/pin/shell-surface tiles. Announced by screen readers when focus enters the grid; not visible. Noun phrase.
shell.dashboard.region.tray: Accessible label for the dashboard's bottom region — the running-window strip plus the clock/status tray. Announced when F6 region navigation moves focus there; not visible. Noun phrase.
shell.dashboard.vaultSwitcher.recoveredA: Button label that re-registers and opens a recovered vault found on disk. Imperative, very short.
shell.dashboard.vaultSwitcher.recoveredH: Heading above a list of vaults found on disk that the app's registry had forgotten (a recovery affordance after registry corruption). Short noun phrase.
shell.errorBoundary.copied: Transient confirmation shown on the copy button after the error details were copied. Very short.
shell.errorBoundary.copy: Button that copies the error details (message + stack) to the clipboard from the crash error boundary. Imperative, short.
shell.errorBoundary.trace: Label/heading for the error stack-trace block shown in the crash error boundary. Short noun phrase.
shell.format.bytes.b: Byte-size label in bytes (e.g. "512 B"). {value} is the numeric amount. Unit abbreviation B is not localised.
shell.format.bytes.gb: Byte-size label in gigabytes (e.g. "3 GB"). {value} is the numeric amount. Unit abbreviation GB is not localised.
shell.format.bytes.kb: Byte-size label in kilobytes (e.g. "4 KB"). {value} is the numeric amount. Unit abbreviation KB is not localised.
shell.format.bytes.mb: Byte-size label in megabytes (e.g. "5 MB"). {value} is the numeric amount. Unit abbreviation MB is not localised.
shell.format.bytes.tb: Byte-size label in terabytes (e.g. "1 TB"). {value} is the numeric amount. Unit abbreviation TB is not localised.
shell.format.daysAgo: Compact relative-time label, N days ago (e.g. "4d ago"). {count} is the whole number of days. Compact form; not pluralised.
shell.format.hoursAgo: Compact relative-time label, N hours ago (e.g. "2h ago"). {count} is the whole number of hours. Compact form; not pluralised.
shell.format.justNow: Relative-time label for a timestamp less than ~10 seconds ago (e.g. a just-completed sync). Shown by the shared formatRelative helper.
shell.format.minutesAgo: Compact relative-time label, N minutes ago (e.g. "3m ago"). {count} is the whole number of minutes. Compact form; not pluralised.
shell.format.secondsAgo: Compact relative-time label, N seconds ago (e.g. "5s ago"). {count} is the whole number of seconds. Compact form; not pluralised.
shell.launcher.openLabel: Accessible label / placeholder for the global launcher search field that searches the whole vault. Very short.
shell.lock.capped: Message shown once the PIN attempt cap is reached and PIN entry is disabled: the only way forward is to close the vault and reopen it with the full passphrase. Sentence; explains the required next step.
shell.lock.closeVault: Label of the button on the capped lock screen that closes the vault (returning to the vault picker, where it can be reopened with the passphrase). Verb phrase, imperative.
shell.lock.cooldown: Status message shown when the user must wait before retrying the PIN after repeated failures. {seconds} is the remaining wait in whole seconds (ICU plural). Sentence.
shell.lock.pinLabel: Accessible label for the masked PIN entry on the lock screen (a segmented 4-box numeric input). 'PIN' = the short numeric code that unlocks the vault (distinct from the full vault passphrase). Noun.
shell.lock.subtitle: One-line instruction under the lock-screen title prompting the user to type their PIN. Sentence.
shell.lock.title: Title of the app-lock screen, the full-screen overlay shown when the vault is locked. Also its accessible dialog label. Short noun phrase; 'vault' is the user's encrypted workspace.
shell.lock.unlock: Label of the button that submits the typed PIN to unlock the vault. Verb, imperative.
shell.lock.wrongPin: Error shown under the PIN field after an incorrect PIN is entered (attempts still remain). Sentence; neutral, not alarming.
shell.marketplace.kindFilter.label: Accessible label for the Marketplace kind-filter tab row (All / Apps / Themes) that narrows the listings grid. Announced by screen readers; not visible. Imperative phrase.
shell.marketplace.region.main: Accessible label for the Marketplace main content region (the right-hand panel showing the active section's cards or detail). Announced by screen readers and on F6 region focus; not visible. Noun phrase.
shell.marketplace.updates.appliedToast.b: Success toast body after an app updates. {name} is the app name, {version} the new version it's now at.
shell.marketplace.updates.appliedToast.t: Success toast title shown after an app updates from the catalog. Short noun phrase.
shell.marketplace.updates.apply: Button label that applies an available update to one installed app. Single word, imperative.
shell.marketplace.updates.consent.body: Body of the consent confirm dialog for an update requesting new permissions. {caps} is a comma-separated list of the new permissions; warns the user to update only a trusted app.
shell.marketplace.updates.consent.title: Title of the confirm dialog shown before applying an update that requests new permissions. {name} is the app's display name.
shell.marketplace.updates.failToast.titl: Error toast title shown when applying an update fails. Short.
shell.marketplace.updates.label: Accessible label for the Marketplace Updates panel list (the available catalog updates for installed apps). Announced by screen readers; not visible. Noun phrase.
shell.marketplace.updates.newCaps: Inline note on an update row listing the new permissions (capabilities) the newer version requests. {caps} is a comma-separated capability list.
shell.marketplace.updates.upToDate: Empty-state message in the Marketplace Updates panel when no installed app has a newer catalog version. Reassuring, short.
shell.notifications.center.clear: Button that clears all notifications in the header notification-center popover. Imperative, short.
shell.notifications.center.empty: Empty-state shown in the notification center when there are no notifications. Short sentence.
shell.notifications.center.title: Heading of the notification-center popover opened from the header bell. Single word.
shell.notifications.openLabel: Accessible label / tooltip for the notification-bell button in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.ai.anthropic.hint: Helper text under the Anthropic provider key field, noting when it's used and where to get a key.
shell.settings.ai.anthropic.name: Label for the Anthropic (Claude) cloud provider in the AI settings panel.
shell.settings.ai.budgetClear: Short label in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Clear”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetCurrent: UI string in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “…”. ICU plural — keep the {count, plural, …} structure.
shell.settings.ai.budgetEdit: UI string in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Edit token budget”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetHint: Helper text in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Set a monthly token ceiling per app. Budgets are recorded now and show”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetNone: UI string in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “No budget”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetPlaceholder: Input placeholder in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “No limit”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetSet: Short label in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Set”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetTitle: Heading in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Per-app token budgets”.
shell.settings.ai.budgetUnit: Unit suffix in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “tokens”.
shell.settings.ai.clear: Button that removes the stored API key for a provider. Verb, imperative. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.gemini.hint: Helper text under the Google Gemini provider key field noting where to get a key.
shell.settings.ai.gemini.name: Label for the Google Gemini cloud provider in the AI settings panel.
shell.settings.ai.glm.hint: Helper text under the z.ai GLM provider key field, noting it is OpenAI-compatible and where to get a key.
shell.settings.ai.glm.name: Label for the z.ai GLM cloud provider in the AI settings panel.
shell.settings.ai.intro: Intro paragraph for the AI providers panel explaining BYO keys are stored encrypted in the vault and the local model needs none.
shell.settings.ai.keyLabel: Field label above the API-key input in the credential dialog. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.keyPlaceholder: Placeholder in the API-key input when no key is set yet. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.mistral.hint: Helper text under the Mistral AI provider key field, noting it is the European OpenAI-compatible provider and where to get a key.
shell.settings.ai.mistral.name: Label for the Mistral AI cloud provider in the AI settings panel.
shell.settings.ai.openai.hint: Helper text under the OpenAI provider key field, noting it also covers OpenAI-compatible endpoints and where to get a key.
shell.settings.ai.openai.name: Label for the OpenAI (and OpenAI-compatible) cloud provider in the AI settings panel.
shell.settings.ai.replacePlaceholder: Placeholder in the API-key input when a key is already configured (typing replaces it). Very short.
shell.settings.ai.routingAuto: UI string in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Automatic (local model)”.
shell.settings.ai.routingHint: Helper text in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Which model handles a request when an app or workflow doesn't pin one.”.
shell.settings.ai.routingTitle: Heading in Settings → AI (provider routing + per-app token budgets): “Default provider”.
shell.settings.ai.save: Button that stores the entered API key for a provider. Verb, imperative. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.statusConfigured: Status chip shown when a provider has an API key stored. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.statusUnset: Status chip shown when a provider has no API key stored. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.title: Heading for the AI providers panel in Settings. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.usageCalls: ICU plural for the number of AI calls an app made. {count} is the call count.
shell.settings.ai.usageHint: Helper text under the AI usage list clarifying it records counts/tokens per app, never prompt or response content.
shell.settings.ai.usageTitle: Heading for the per-app AI usage summary in the AI settings panel. Very short.
shell.settings.ai.usageTokens: ICU plural for the total tokens an app's AI calls used. {count} is the token total.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.ac: Label for the button that starts a whole-vault export (opens a save dialog). Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.do: Confirmation after a successful export. {path} is the file path the bundle was written to.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.fa: Generic error shown when a vault export fails. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.hi: Explanatory text under the Export heading: writes the entire vault to a single .bsbundle file for backup or device-move.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.ti: Heading for the 'export the whole vault to a file' group in Backup & Migration. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ag: Label for the button that resets the import flow to start another import. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ca: Label for the button that cancels the in-progress import flow. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.do: Completion summary after import. {created}/{updated}/{failed} are integer counts of entities created, updated, and failed.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.hi: Explanatory text under the Import heading: import rows from a JSON/JSONL/CSV/Markdown/HTML file with a non-destructive preview first.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ma: Column header for the source column name in the import mapping table. Very short.
Column header for the per-column 'import this column?' checkbox in the import mapping table. Very short.
Accessible label for a row's include checkbox. {column} is the source column name.
Column header for the target vault property name in the import mapping table. Very short.
Accessible label for a row's target-property input. {column} is the source column name.
Column header for the inferred value type in the import mapping table. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.pi: Label for the button that opens a file picker to choose a data file to import. Very short.
Error shown when the chosen import file cannot be read. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.pl: Dry-run summary before importing. {create}/{update}/{total} are integer counts of records that would be created, updated, and total.
Error shown when building the import dry-run preview fails. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.pr: Label for the button that runs a non-destructive dry-run of the import (shows counts). Very short.
Live progress shown while an import runs. {done} and {total} are integer counts of entities committed / total.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ru: Label for the button that commits the import. Very short.
Generic error shown when committing the import fails. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.so: Summary of the picked import file. {file} is the file name; {count} is the integer number of records found.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.st: Label for the button that cancels an in-progress import. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ta: Hint under the target-type field giving an example entity type (e.g. brainstorm/Note/v1).
Field label for the vault entity type the imported rows will map onto. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ti: Heading for the 'import data from a file' group in Backup & Migration. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ty: Validation message shown when the user tries to preview/import without entering a target type.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.hi: Explanatory text under the Notion heading: a Markdown & CSV export zip becomes pages + database rows + parent links, re-import updates not duplicates.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.pi: Label for the button that opens a file picker to choose a Notion export zip. Very short.
Error shown when the chosen Notion export zip cannot be read. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.ru: Label for the button that commits the Notion export import. Very short.
Generic error shown when a Notion export import fails. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.so: Summary of the picked Notion export. {archive} is the zip file name; {count} is the integer number of pages found.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.ta: Hint under the Notion target-type field giving an example entity type each page becomes.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.ti: Heading for the 'import a Notion export' group in Backup & Migration. Very short.
Explanatory text under the Obsidian heading: frontmatter becomes properties, wikilinks/embeds become links, tags are kept, re-import updates not duplicates.
Label for the button that opens a folder picker to choose an Obsidian vault. Very short.
Error shown when the chosen Obsidian folder cannot be read. Very short.
Label for the button that commits the Obsidian vault import. Very short.
Generic error shown when an Obsidian vault import fails. Very short.
Summary of the picked Obsidian folder. {folder} is the folder name; {count} is the integer number of notes found.
Hint under the Obsidian target-type field giving an example entity type each note becomes.
Heading for the 'import an Obsidian vault folder' group in Backup & Migration. Very short.
shell.settings.backupMigration.summary: One-line summary at the top of the Backup & Migration settings panel describing export + import.
shell.settings.contributions.contributes: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions: “Adds actions to other apps' menus”.
shell.settings.contributions.empty: Empty-state text in Settings → Apps & contributions: “No apps installed.”.
shell.settings.contributions.loading: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions: “Loading apps…”.
shell.settings.contributions.none: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions: “No cross-app actions”.
shell.settings.contributions.summary: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions: “Installed apps can add actions to other apps' menus — like Summarize o”.
shell.settings.contributions.toggle: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions: “Allow {app} to add actions to other apps' menus”. Interpolates {app}.
shell.settings.data.dictionaries.itemCou: Count of items in a dictionary (vocabulary), shown on the dictionary row in Settings → Data → Dictionaries. {count} is the integer item total; ICU plural picks 'item'/'items'.
shell.settings.data.properties.allowedTy: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Anything”.
Empty-state text in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “No object types are registered yet — this property will link to anythi”.
Helper text in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Choose which object types this property can link to. Leave none select”.
Field label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Links to”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.amb: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Amber”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.blu: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Blue”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.cya: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Cyan”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.gre: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Green”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.ora: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Orange”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.pin: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Pink”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.pur: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Purple”.
shell.settings.data.properties.color.red: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Red”.
shell.settings.data.properties.deleteCon: Cautionary note in the delete-property confirmation dialog, stating how many objects use the property whose values will be orphaned. {count} is the integer usage total; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.settings.data.properties.itemUsage: Pill on a dictionary value showing how many objects currently hold that value. {count} is the integer count; the whole clause is pluralized so the verb agrees ('uses'/'use').
shell.settings.data.properties.kindBadge: Short label in Settings → Data (vault properties / dictionaries): “Formula”.
shell.settings.data.properties.usagePill: Pill on a property row showing how many objects currently use that property, in Settings → Data → Properties. {count} is the integer usage total; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.settings.files.daysAgo: Relative time for when a file handle was last accessed, in days, in Settings → Security → Open files. {count} is the whole-day count; ICU plural picks 'day'/'days'.
shell.settings.files.hoursAgo: Relative time for when a file handle was last accessed, in hours, in Settings → Security → Open files. {count} is the whole-hour count; ICU plural picks 'hour'/'hours'.
shell.settings.files.minutesAgo: Relative time for when a file handle was last accessed, in minutes, in Settings → Security → Open files. {count} is the whole-minute count; ICU plural picks 'minute'/'minutes'.
shell.settings.identity.avatarEdit: UI string in Settings → Identity: “Change your avatar”.
shell.settings.identity.displayName: UI string in Settings → Identity: “Display name”.
shell.settings.identity.displayNameHint: Helper text in Settings → Identity: “The name collaborators see for you across shared channels, comments, a”.
shell.settings.identity.displayNamePlace: Input placeholder in Settings → Identity: “How you appear to others”.
shell.settings.identity.fingerprint: UI string in Settings → Identity: “Identity fingerprint”.
shell.settings.identity.fingerprintHint: Helper text in Settings → Identity: “Your sovereign public-key fingerprint. Compare it out-of-band with a c”.
shell.settings.identity.noVault: UI string in Settings → Identity: “No vault open”.
shell.settings.interface.clock.hourCycle: Label for the 12/24-hour clock-format picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.clock.seconds: Toggle label for showing seconds on the dashboard clock. Short phrase.
shell.settings.interface.clock.show: Toggle label for showing the dashboard clock. Short imperative phrase.
shell.settings.interface.clock.title: Heading for the clock options group in Settings → Interface. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.addWidg: UI string in Settings → Interface: “Add widget”.
shell.settings.interface.control.appeara: Toggle label for the light/dark appearance control in the dashboard header. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.control.bin: Toggle label for the Bin (trash) control in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.cheatsh: Toggle label for the keyboard-shortcut cheatsheet control in the dashboard header. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.control.clock: Toggle label for showing the clock control in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.help: Toggle label for the Help control in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.marketp: Toggle label for the Marketplace control in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.notific: Toggle label for the notifications bell control in the dashboard header. Single word.
shell.settings.interface.control.search: Label for the in-app find/search control toggle in Settings → Interface. Very short.
shell.settings.interface.control.setting: Toggle label for the Settings control in the dashboard header. Single word.
Note explaining the Settings control can't be hidden because it's how the user reaches this screen. Short sentence.
shell.settings.interface.control.syncSta: Toggle label for the sync-status control in the dashboard header. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.control.vaultIn: Toggle label for the vault-info control in the dashboard header. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.controls.summar: One-line summary under the header-controls heading: choose which controls show in the dashboard header. Sentence.
shell.settings.interface.controls.title: Heading for the header-controls visibility list in Settings → Interface. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.interface.reset: Button that resets all Interface settings to their defaults. Imperative, short.
shell.settings.keyboard.action.search: Name of the 'search the vault' keyboard action shown in the Settings → Keyboard shortcuts list. Very short.
shell.settings.loading: Generic loading placeholder shown while a settings section's data resolves. Short; ends with an ellipsis.
shell.settings.mcp.add: Button label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “+ Add server”.
shell.settings.mcp.addTitle: Heading in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Add MCP server”.
shell.settings.mcp.approveChanges: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Approve changes”.
shell.settings.mcp.argsLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Arguments (one per line)”.
shell.settings.mcp.authLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Auth token”.
shell.settings.mcp.authPlaceholder: Input placeholder in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Bearer token”.
shell.settings.mcp.authReplacePlaceholde: Input placeholder in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Enter a new token to replace the stored one”.
shell.settings.mcp.commandLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Command”.
shell.settings.mcp.commandPlaceholder: Input placeholder in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “npx”.
shell.settings.mcp.destructive: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “destructive”.
shell.settings.mcp.disable: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Disable”.
shell.settings.mcp.enable: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Enable”.
shell.settings.mcp.idLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Server id”.
shell.settings.mcp.idPlaceholder: Input placeholder in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “e.g. github”.

shell.settings.mcp.inspecting: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Discovering tools…”.
shell.settings.mcp.intro: Helper text in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Connect external Model Context Protocol servers so the Agent can use t”.
shell.settings.mcp.nameLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Name”.
shell.settings.mcp.namePlaceholder: Input placeholder in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “GitHub”.
shell.settings.mcp.readOnly: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “read-only”.
shell.settings.mcp.remove: Button label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Remove”.
shell.settings.mcp.requiresAuth: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Requires an auth token”.
shell.settings.mcp.save: Button label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Save”.
shell.settings.mcp.spawnConsentNote: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “This runs a program on your computer. An agent can only launch it afte”.
shell.settings.mcp.statusDisabled: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Disabled”.
shell.settings.mcp.statusEnabled: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Enabled”.
shell.settings.mcp.title: Heading in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “MCP servers”.
shell.settings.mcp.toolChanged: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “changed”.
shell.settings.mcp.toolNew: Short label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “new”.
shell.settings.mcp.transport.http: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Streamable HTTP”.
shell.settings.mcp.transport.sse: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Server-Sent Events”.
shell.settings.mcp.transport.stdio: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Local process (stdio)”.
shell.settings.mcp.transportLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Transport”.
shell.settings.mcp.untrustedNote: UI string in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Tool names and descriptions below are provided by the server and are u”.
shell.settings.mcp.urlLabel: Field label in Settings → Apps & contributions → MCP servers (Model Context Protocol): “Endpoint URL”.
shell.settings.network.automation.add: Button label in Settings → Network: “Add host”.
shell.settings.network.automation.empty: Empty-state text in Settings → Network: “No hosts approved — Automations HTTP steps are blocked until you add o”.
shell.settings.network.automation.revoke: UI string in Settings → Network: “Automations will no longer be able to send requests to this host.”.
shell.settings.network.automation.title: Heading in Settings → Network: “Automation network access”.
shell.settings.network.automation.warnin: UI string in Settings → Network: “Hosts your Automations may send requests to. An automation granted a h”.
shell.settings.network.crash.localCount: Status line stating how many crash reports were captured locally before the user opted in, in Settings → Privacy → Network → Crash reporter. {count} is the integer total; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.settings.network.crash.pendingCoun: Status line stating how many crash reports are queued for submission, in Settings → Privacy → Network → Crash reporter. {count} is the integer pending total; ICU plural picks 'report'/'reports'.
shell.settings.network.perApp.requestCou: Count of network requests an app has made, in the Settings → Privacy → Network per-app egress table. {count} is the integer request total; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.settings.notifications.center.clea: Button that clears all notifications from the center. Imperative, short.
shell.settings.notifications.center.coun: Status line counting total + unread notifications. {count} total, {unread} unread; ICU plural picks the noun form.
shell.settings.notifications.center.titl: Heading for the notification-center group in Settings → Notifications. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.notifications.delivery.ti: Heading for the notification-delivery group in Settings → Notifications. Single word.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.enable: Toggle label for enabling quiet hours (do not disturb). Short phrase.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.end: Label for the quiet-hours end-time field. Single word.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.start: Label for the quiet-hours start-time field. Single word.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.summary: One-line summary: mute popups during set hours; alerts still reach the notification center. Sentence.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.title: Heading for the do-not-disturb group in Settings → Notifications. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.notifications.osNative.de: One-line note under the OS-notifications toggle explaining alerts also appear in the operating system. Sentence.
shell.settings.notifications.osNative.la: Toggle label for also showing alerts in the OS notification center. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.notifications.perApp.empt: Empty-state shown when no apps are installed to configure. Short sentence.
shell.settings.notifications.perApp.summ: One-line summary: turn notifications off for individual apps. Sentence.
shell.settings.notifications.perApp.titl: Heading for the per-app notification toggles in Settings → Notifications. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.notifications.perApp.togg: Accessible label for an app's notification toggle. {app} is the app name.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.items.cou: Count of restorable items in Recently Deleted. {count} is the integer total; ICU plural picks the =0 / one / other wording.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.items.tit: Heading for the list of deleted items in the Recently Deleted settings section. Very short.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.openBin: Label for the button that opens the Recently Deleted overlay. Very short.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.retention: Retention-period option: keep deleted items 30 days. Very short.
Retention-period option: keep deleted items one year. Very short.
Retention-period option: keep deleted items 7 days. Very short.
Retention-period option: keep deleted items 90 days. Very short.
Helper text under the retention-period control explaining that items older than the window are permanently removed when Recently Deleted is next opened.
Option label for keeping deleted items indefinitely (no retention limit). Very short.
Label for the control that sets how long deleted items are kept before permanent removal. Very short.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.summary: Summary at the top of the Recently Deleted settings section explaining the cross-app restore window.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.auto: Date-format option: follow the system automatically. Single word.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.full: Date-format option: full style (e.g. Tuesday, June 9, 2026). Single word.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.label: Label for the date-format picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.long: Date-format option: long style (e.g. June 9, 2026). Single word.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.medium: Date-format option: medium style (e.g. Jun 9, 2026). Single word.
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.short: Date-format option: short style (e.g. 6/9/26). Single word.
shell.settings.region.firstDay.auto: First-day-of-week option: follow the system automatically. Single word.
shell.settings.region.firstDay.label: Label for the first-day-of-week picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.formats.summary: One-line summary: override date/time/number formatting, or follow the system. Sentence.
shell.settings.region.formats.title: Heading for the regional-formats group in Settings → Language & Region. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.hourCycle.auto: Picker option: follow the system time format automatically. Single word.
shell.settings.region.hourCycle.h12: Picker option for 12-hour time format. Short.
shell.settings.region.hourCycle.h23: Picker option for 24-hour time format. Short.
shell.settings.region.hourCycle.label: Label for the time-format (12/24-hour) picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.language.label: Label for the display-language picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.language.machineBa: Badge marking a language pack as machine-translated (not human-reviewed). Short.
shell.settings.region.language.summary: One-line summary under the language heading: the UI language, applied immediately. Sentence.
shell.settings.region.language.title: Heading for the display-language group in Settings → Language & Region. Single word.
shell.settings.region.main: Accessible label for the Settings main content region (the right-hand panel showing the active section). Announced by screen readers and on F6 region focus; not visible. Noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.number.auto: Number-format option: follow the system automatically. Single word.
shell.settings.region.number.label: Label for the number-format picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.region.timezone.auto: Time-zone option: follow the system time zone automatically. Short phrase.
shell.settings.region.timezone.label: Label for the time-zone picker. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.section.ai: Sidebar label for the AI settings section (provider keys). Very short.
shell.settings.section.backupMigration: Sidebar label for the Settings → Backup & Migration section (vault export + data import). Very short.
shell.settings.section.contributions: UI string in Settings sidebar section label: “Apps & contributions”.
shell.settings.section.identity: Short label in Settings sidebar section label: “Identity”.
shell.settings.section.interface: Sidebar label for the Interface settings section (header controls + clock). Single word.
shell.settings.section.languageRegion: Sidebar label for the Language & Region settings section. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.section.notifications: Sidebar label for the Notifications settings section. Single word.
shell.settings.section.recentlyDeleted: Sidebar label for the Settings → Recently Deleted section. Very short.
shell.settings.security.appLock.autoLock: Dropdown option for the auto-lock idle interval. {count} is the number of idle minutes before locking (ICU plural). Short phrase.
Dropdown option meaning the vault never auto-locks (it only locks manually). Single word.
Label for the dropdown choosing how long of an idle period triggers an automatic lock. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.security.appLock.changePi: Button that opens the form to replace the existing PIN with a new one. Verb phrase, imperative.
shell.settings.security.appLock.confirmP: Label above the second PIN entry that must match the first (typo guard). Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.security.appLock.descript: One-line explanation under the App-lock heading: a PIN is required to unlock the vault once it has locked. Sentence.
shell.settings.security.appLock.invalid: Inline error shown when the typed PIN is not 6 digits. Short sentence, instructive.
shell.settings.security.appLock.lockNow: Button that immediately locks the vault, raising the lock screen. Verb phrase, imperative.
shell.settings.security.appLock.mismatch: Inline error shown when the two PIN fields differ. Short sentence.
shell.settings.security.appLock.pinPlace: Label above the PIN entry (a segmented 6-box numeric input). States the accepted shape: a 6-digit PIN. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.security.appLock.removeCo: Body of the remove-PIN confirmation: explains the vault will no longer require a PIN. Sentence.
Title of the confirmation dialog before removing the PIN lock. Short question.
shell.settings.security.appLock.removePi: Button (and the destructive confirm label) that deletes the PIN, disabling the app lock. Verb phrase, imperative.
shell.settings.security.appLock.setPin: Button that opens the form to set a PIN for the first time. Verb phrase, imperative.
shell.settings.security.appLock.statusOf: Status line shown when no PIN lock is configured. Short sentence.
shell.settings.security.appLock.statusOn: Status line shown when a PIN lock is currently configured for this vault. Short sentence.
shell.settings.security.appLock.title: Heading of the App-lock subsection in Settings → Security. 'App lock' = the optional PIN gate that masks the vault behind a lock screen. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.security.permissionCount: Count of capabilities an app has been granted, in the Settings → Security grants list. {count} is the integer number of permissions; ICU plural picks the singular/plural noun.
shell.settings.spellcheck.empty: Empty-state text for the custom spellcheck dictionary when no words have been added yet.
shell.settings.spellcheck.languages: States which languages spelling is being checked in. {langs} is a comma-separated language-name list.
shell.settings.spellcheck.languagesAuto: Note that spelling languages are auto-detected by the operating system (shown when no explicit language is set).
shell.settings.spellcheck.remove: Accessible label for the button removing a word from the custom dictionary. {word} is the dictionary word.
shell.settings.spellcheck.title: Heading for the custom spellcheck dictionary manager in Settings. Very short.
shell.settings.sync.policy.mode.label: Field label in Settings → Sync: “Sync”.
shell.settings.sync.policy.mode.pinned: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Pinned only”.
shell.settings.sync.policy.mode.pinnedPl: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Pinned + recent”.
shell.settings.sync.policy.recentDays.la: Field label in Settings → Sync: “Recent window (days)”.
shell.settings.sync.policy.summary: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Choose which shared items this device keeps in sync. Items left out ar”.
shell.settings.sync.restore.action: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Restore from sync”.
shell.settings.sync.restore.done: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Restored {restored} of …”. ICU plural — keep the {count, plural, …} structure. Interpolates {restored}.
shell.settings.sync.restore.failed: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Restore couldn't complete. Check your connection and try again.”.
shell.settings.sync.restore.summary: UI string in Settings → Sync: “This device is empty but signed in. Recover your synced items from the”.
shell.settings.sync.restore.title: Heading in Settings → Sync: “Restore from sync”.
shell.settings.sync.section.policy: UI string in Settings → Sync: “Selective sync”.
shell.settings.themes.aurora.description: Description of the 'Aurora' theme (deep violet surfaces, fuchsia accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.aurora.label: Display name of the 'Aurora' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.themes.forest.description: Description of the 'Forest' theme (deep evergreen surfaces, emerald accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.forest.label: Display name of the 'Forest' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.themes.mint.description: Description of the 'Mint' theme (light green-tinted surfaces, teal accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.mint.label: Display name of the 'Mint' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.themes.nord.description: Description of the 'Nord' theme (arctic blue-grey surfaces, frost-blue accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.nord.label: Display name of the 'Nord' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.themes.rose.description: Description of the 'Rose' theme (blush surfaces, warm rose accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.rose.label: Display name of the 'Rose' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.themes.slate.description: Description of the 'Slate' theme (neutral-grey surfaces, indigo accent) in the theme picker.
shell.settings.themes.slate.label: Display name of the 'Slate' theme. Very short; a proper noun, may stay untranslated.
shell.settings.updates.available: Status line shown when a newer version exists. {version} is the available version number.
shell.settings.updates.channel: Row label for the release-channel picker (Stable vs Beta). Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.updates.channel.beta: Option label for the beta (pre-release) channel. Single word.
shell.settings.updates.channel.descripti: One-line note under the release-channel picker: Beta gets newer, possibly less stable builds. Sentence.
shell.settings.updates.channel.stable: Option label for the stable release channel. Single word.
shell.settings.updates.check: Button that checks the release feed for a newer version. Imperative, short.
shell.settings.updates.checking: Button label while a check is in progress. Short; ends with an ellipsis.
shell.settings.updates.currentVersion: Row label showing the running app version number. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.updates.download: Button that starts downloading the update (packaged build) or opens the release page (dev). Imperative, short.
shell.settings.updates.downloading: Status line shown while the update binary downloads. {percent} is the integer download percentage.
shell.settings.updates.error: Status line shown when an update check or download failed. {message} is the failure detail.
shell.settings.updates.lastChecked: Caption showing when the last update check ran. {when} is a localized date-time string.
shell.settings.updates.restartInstall: Button that quits and installs the downloaded update, relaunching the app. Imperative, short.
shell.settings.updates.summary: One-line explanation under the Updates heading: check for a newer Brainstorm version, then download and install it. Sentence.
shell.settings.updates.title: Heading of the Updates subsection in Settings → General. Short noun.
shell.settings.updates.updateReady: Status line shown when an update has finished downloading and a relaunch will install it. {version} is the version number. Sentence.
shell.settings.updates.unknown: Status line shown when the check couldn't complete (offline / bad feed). Short sentence; second clause imperative.
shell.settings.updates.upToDate: Status line shown when the running version is already the newest. Short sentence.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.col.blo: Column header in the browser per-host egress table: how many requests to the host the tracker blocklist cancelled. One word.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.col.hos: Column header in the browser per-host egress table: the contacted host name. One word.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.col.las: Column header in the browser per-host egress table: relative time of the most recent request to the host. Very short.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.col.req: Column header in the browser per-host egress table: how many requests reached the host. One word.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.empty: Empty state for the browser per-host egress table when no in-app-browser traffic has been recorded yet. Short sentence.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.summary: One-line explanation under the browser-egress heading: rows are hosts contacted by pages in the in-app browser; only counts are kept, never addresses or page content. Two short sentences.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.title: Heading of the Settings → Privacy group listing per-host traffic from the in-app browser. Short noun phrase.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permission.cam: Device-permission kind label shown on a browser site-permission row: the site may use the camera. One word, noun.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permission.geo: Device-permission kind label shown on a browser site-permission row: the site may read the user's location. One word, noun.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permission.mic: Device-permission kind label shown on a browser site-permission row: the site may use the microphone. One word, noun.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.al: Status pill on a browser site-permission row when the user explicitly allowed the device for that site. One word, past participle.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.bl: Status pill on a browser site-permission row when the user explicitly blocked the device for that site (the site stops asking). One word, past participle.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.em: Empty state for the browser site-permissions list when the user has not allowed or blocked any site yet; reassures that the browser asks before any device use. Short sentence.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.re: Accessible label of the icon button that removes every device decision for a site, returning it to ask-before-use. Imperative, short.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.su: One-line explanation under the browser site-permissions heading: these are the camera/microphone/location decisions taken in the in-app browser; everything else is denied by default. Two short sentences.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.ti: Heading of the Settings → Privacy group listing per-site device-permission decisions from the in-app browser. Short noun phrase.
shell.vaultRecovery.dismiss: Button that closes the corruption-recovery modal without changing anything (the user will restore from a backup and reopen instead). The SAFE default. Short.
shell.vaultRecovery.rebuild: Button that rebuilds a corrupt entities database from the vault's synced content. Imperative, short.
shell.vaultRecovery.reinitialize: Destructive button that resets a corrupt, unrecoverable database (capability ledger / vault registry) to an empty one — grants and metadata are lost. Imperative, short.
shell.vaultRecovery.title: Title of the modal shown when a vault fails to open because one of its databases is corrupt. Short sentence.
shell.welcome.continue: Label for the button advancing to the next step of the first-run welcome flow. Very short.
shell.welcome.createFailed: Error shown when creating the vault fails during onboarding.
shell.welcome.migrate.cta: First-launch menu button that opens the 'Migrating from…' picker (Obsidian / Notion / files). Verb phrase, short.
shell.welcome.migrate.files: Label for the generic-files migration source in the 'Migrating from…' picker (Markdown / HTML / CSV / JSON / JSONL).
shell.welcome.migrate.hint: Helper text in the migrate picker explaining the user first creates a vault and the import wizard then opens automatically. One short sentence.
shell.welcome.migrate.notion: Label for the Notion migration source in the 'Migrating from…' picker (Notion's Markdown & CSV / HTML export).
shell.welcome.migrate.obsidian: Label for the Obsidian migration source in the 'Migrating from…' picker.
shell.welcome.migrate.start: Primary button in the migrate picker that begins vault creation so the user has a vault to import into. Verb phrase, short.
shell.welcome.migrate.subtitle: Subtitle under the 'Migrating from…' title explaining data comes in as native objects with types/properties/links preserved.
shell.welcome.migrate.title: Title of the first-launch migration picker popover. Very short.
shell.welcome.nameTaken: Inline error on the create-vault form when the entered name collides with an existing vault (case- and whitespace-insensitive). {name} is the entered name. Short; the second clause is imperative.
shell.welcome.openFailed: Error shown when opening the newly-created vault fails during onboarding.
shell.welcome.skip: Label for the button that skips the optional starter-content step of onboarding. Very short.
shell.welcome.startStepSubtitle: Subtitle for the onboarding 'choose a starting point' step (example notes / template / empty vault).
shell.welcome.startStepTitle: Title for the onboarding step where the user picks how to start their vault. Very short.
shell.welcome.starterContentHint: Helper text under the starter-content checkbox explaining what gets added and that it is editable/removable. One short sentence.
shell.welcome.starterContentLabel: Label for a checkbox on the create-vault form that, when checked (the default), adds first-launch example content to the new vault. Very short.
shell.welcome.starterContentTemplateHint: Hint shown when a use-case template is selected, noting it replaces the default example content and can be edited or removed later.
shell.welcome.templates.heading: Heading above the use-case template gallery in onboarding. Very short.
shell.welcome.templates.hint: Helper text under the template gallery explaining a template is added as its own removable collection.
shell.welcome.templates.importFailedBody: Body of the notice shown when the vault was created but the chosen template failed to import.
shell.welcome.templates.importFailedTitl: Title of the notice shown when a starter template fails to import during onboarding. Very short.
shell.widgets.add.empty: Empty-state text in the dashboard widgets layer: “No widgets available”.
shell.widgets.add.label: Field label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Add widget”.
shell.widgets.collapse: Button label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Collapse widget”.
shell.widgets.expand: Button label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Expand widget”.
shell.widgets.layerLabel: Field label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Dashboard widgets”.
shell.widgets.menu.label: Field label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Widget options”.
shell.widgets.menu.remove: Button label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Remove widget”.
shell.widgets.open: Button label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Open {name}”. Interpolates {name}.
shell.widgets.resize: Button label in the dashboard widgets layer: “Resize widget”.
shell.windowMenu.region: Accessible label for the running-windows strip / window-switcher toolbar region in the dashboard footer. Very short.
shell.windowStrip.scrollLeft: Button label in the dashboard window strip (open-window switcher): “Scroll windows left”.
shell.windowStrip.scrollRight: Button label in the dashboard window strip (open-window switcher): “Scroll windows right”.
